extracted_questions.question: The first law of thermodynamics implies that living organisms cannot create their own energy but can only convert one form of energy into another. What, then, is the ultimate source of energy for most living organisms?

choices.A: Chemical energy from the glucose molecule made by plants during photosynthesis
choices.B: The chemical energy released by the numerous hydrolytic reactions in a cell
choices.E: ATP made in the mitochondria of both plants and animals
